candidate_problems.text: What is the actual selection mechanism over possible law-sets, and in what sense can only complexity-supporting universes become 'visible' without smuggling in an observer-selection tautology? The conjecture invokes repeated selection and equilibrium over law-space, but it is unresolved whether this is a real dynamical process, a multiverse measure claim, or merely a redescription of anthropic filtering.

Can the game-theoretic notions of repeated interaction, memory, legibility, and propagated consequences be made into rigorous, quantitative criteria on physical law-sets that predict complexity-supporting parameter ranges? The open problem is to translate the metaphor into testable invariants or metrics and show they recover known fine-tuning constraints better than existing stability, entropy-flow, and habitability analyses.

Is stable complexity genuinely narrow in parameter space for deep structural reasons, or does the conjecture overstate fine-tuning by assuming our form of cumulative organization is the only relevant kind? A key unresolved tension is whether alternative laws could support different substrates of memory, persistence, and self-amplification, which would weaken the claim that the 'cooperative regime' is exceptionally rare.
